# NeuroWorks AI4I Proposal

__NeuroWorks__

*The Intelligent Organisation Platform*

*Turn job descriptions into AI workers\. Design the intelligent organisation\. Reorganise intelligence itself\.*

__Project Title:  __NeuroWorks AI Digital Workforce and Intelligent Organisation Platform

__Track Name:  __Track 3 Development

__Team Name:  __NeuroWorks

__Lead Innovator:  __Aiia & Rubiem  
__Date:  __11 July 2026

__Repository:  __github\.com/RUBIEM\-DEVELOPERS\-REPO/neuroworks 

__Demo:  __Live demonstration  Day 5, Friday 31 July 2026

![aiia plain](data:image/jpeg;base64,)![rubiem logo](data:image/png;base64,)

# __Section 1 Problem Definition and Strategic Alignment__

## __1\.1 Four Structural Failures Defining the Problem__

The organisational capacity crisis in Zimbabwe is not a technology problem or a skills problem in isolation\. It is a structural design problem\. The organisation itself as a concept was built for the industrial era: fixed headcount, hierarchical org charts, job descriptions tied to qualifications, hiring and scaling measured in months and years\. That model is failing four ways simultaneously\.

__1\. Intelligent organisation gap__

There is no established model for what an AI\-age adaptive intelligent organisation looks like\. Institutions are attempting to bolt AI features onto industrial\-era structures rather than redesigning the organisation itself\.

__2\. Labour cost and scaling gap__

Hiring and scaling human workers is expensive and slow\. A rural clinic, an NGO, a government department none can grow their knowledge\-work capacity at the speed their mandate requires\. The cost of adding one qualified specialist can consume an entire department's discretionary budget\.

__3\. Workforce capacity gap__

The volume of knowledge work researching, drafting, querying, coordinating, reporting, complying structurally exceeds what any affordable number of hires can cover\. A single NGO programme officer performs the work of five specialists simultaneously\. Quality suffers\. Deadlines slip\. Compliance fails\.

__4\. Jobs and org structures are industrial\-era artefacts__

Job descriptions, qualifications, and org charts were invented to manage industrial labour\. The AI age demands a different unit of organisational design: skills, outcomes, and coordinated intelligence not headcount\. The organisations that understand this first will operate at an entirely different level\.

__NeuroWorks' answer: design your own man\-machine organisation\. Define the roles, the policies, the outcomes\. NeuroWorks turns job descriptions into AI workers that perform those roles at scale briefed like people, governed by the organisation's own policies, supervised by humans, and learning continuously from the organisation's own data through IntelliNexus\.__

## __1\.2 The Intelligent Organisation, What It Looks Like__

The intelligent organisation does not replace humans with AI\. It reorganises how intelligence is deployed so that human capacity is directed at judgment, relationships, and decisions that genuinely require it while AI workers handle the information\-based, tool\-mediated portion of knowledge work that currently consumes most of an organisation's time\. It is not a hybrid of the old model and a new tool\. It is a different design entirely\.

NeuroWorks digital workers are not tools\. A tool waits to be used\. A NeuroWorks digital worker receives a goal, forms a plan, calls the tools it needs, checks every action against the organisation's uploaded policy constraints, and pauses for human approval before anything consequential executes\. The human stays in control of judgment\. The worker handles execution\. Brief it the way you brief a person\.

## __1\.3 Target Users and Beneficiaries__

NeuroWorks is an operational platform, not a consumer product\. Its 12 built\-in sector context packs name the intended Zimbabwe\-market users with real institutional detail:

__Sector__

__Named target users and institutions__

Finance and fintech

Reconciliation desks, agent\-network managers RBZ framework, EcoCash, OneMoney mobile money infrastructure

Agriculture

Field\-reporting teams, input\-financing coordinators Pfumvudza/Intwasa government smallholder programme, GMB

Health

Community health worker coordinators, stock\-out analysts MOHCC, DHIS2\-integrated facilities

Education

Admin offices, reporting staff ZIMSEC exam board, 2\-7\-4\-2\-4 curriculum institutions

Tourism

Booking desks, intelligence teams ZTA \(Zimbabwe Tourism Authority\), UNIVISA/KAZA cross\-border scheme

Public services

Procurement compliance, records management PRAZ, devolution\-agenda departments, ZIMRA

Informal economy / retail

Market operators Mbare Musika, kopje trading, USD/ZWL dual pricing, high\-density delivery logistics

Development / NGO

Grant reporting, donor coordination WFP, WHO, UNICEF, UNDP reporting cadences

__Scope boundary__

Fisheries, future skills, and energy do not have dedicated sector packs today\. Stated plainly rather than stretched to fit\.

## __1\.4 Strategic Alignment__

### __National AI Strategy__

Three of the five priorities have direct, evidenced fit\. Sovereign AI infrastructure: NeuroWorks runs on Ollama \(local, open\-weight model\) by default zero API keys, no mandatory foreign cloud dependency\. Data never leaves the machine in default operation; 62\.7% of all LLM calls in the live 10\-day window were served free and locally\. MSME and informal economy inclusion: the retail sector pack targets informal\-market operators specifically; native Paynow Zimbabwe integration lowers the digital\-payment barrier\. Public sector efficiency: the public\-sector pack names PRAZ compliance explicitly; the governance engine blocks a non\-compliant action before it executes, not after\. National data assets is a partial fit: the IntelliNexus pipeline is architecturally capable of building governed, hash\-verified institutional datasets at scale but today operates at single\-operator scale\. AI skills development is not claimed\.

### __NDS2 Pillars__

Pillar 3 \(Governance and Institutions\): strongest claim pre\-execution policy enforcement, named PRAZ and devolution focus, full audit trail\. Pillar 1 \(Economic Growth and Stability\): real productivity gains in fintech, agriculture, tourism, and public services\. Pillar 2 \(Social Development\): partial fit through health\-pack and education\-pack admin efficiency\. Pillars 4 and 5 are not claimed\.

# __Section 2 Technical Design and Product Logic__

## __2\.1 System Architecture__

NeuroWorks operates as three layers\. At the bottom: tools databases, email, GitHub, payment systems, file stores\. In the middle: HERMES, the MCP tool\-governance layer an allowlisted set of approximately 90 primitives defining exactly what agents can and cannot do\. At the top: digital workers LLM\-powered agents that receive goals, form plans, and call tools through HERMES under mandatory human oversight at every consequential step\.

__Backend__

Node\.js / TypeScript, Express 28 live routers, lib/ \(85 business\-logic files\), routes/ \(46 handlers\)\. Every route has a real handler; no scaffolding stubs\.

__Frontend__

Next\.js 14, React, TypeScript 37 pages: operator console, Mission Control, Knowledge Packs browser, cost dashboard, governance policy manager\.

__Agent orchestration__

LangGraph, multi\-agent via EXECUTOR inter\-agent communication, HITL gate management, structured audit logging on every action\.

__Local inference__

Ollama, always\-on, zero API key\. Default path\. No data leaves the machine\. Planning, synthesis, extraction all local\.

__Cloud inference__

OpenRouter \+ circuit breaker \+ billing\-exhaustion fallback to Ollama\. Claude / Anthropic cost\-tiered\. Opt\-in only\.

__Vector store \(RAG\)__

Qdrant\. organisation\-isolated collections, HNSW indexing, snapshot export for portability\.

__Relational database__

PostgreSQL — users, orgs, job definitions, audit logs, billing, connector configs\.

__Cache / agent state__

Redis — working memory and job queues during execution; persisted to PostgreSQL on mission completion\.

__Secrets__

AES\-256\-GCM encrypted \.neuroworks store \(secret\-box\.ts\)\. Credentials never logged or passed by value into agent context\.

__IntelliNexus __

7\-stage data pipeline: normalise → SHA\-256 hash \(Merkle root per batch\) → confidence score → HITL review queue → entity resolution → publish \(4 artifact formats\) → vault\-index for agent RAG retrieval\. Synchronised human and machine learning from the same institutional data\.

__Payment rails__

Stripe \(global\) and Paynow Zimbabwe \(local\) — both live\-tested\. Only platform with a Zimbabwe\-market local payment rail already wired\.

## __2\.2 AI Models and Justification__

The tasks NeuroWorks performs are genuinely irreducible to simpler logic: synthesising a reconciliation report from 24 database tables cross\-checked against a natural\-language policy document; interpreting donor reporting requirements from an uploaded PDF and drafting a compliant narrative; responding to a customer query in Shona within the organisation's service\-policy boundaries\. None of these are achievable with SQL or rule\-based logic\. They require language understanding, multi\-step planning, and context retention\.

__Model / method__

__Role__

__Why__

Ollama — Llama 3\.2 3B / Qwen 2\.5 7B \(local\)

Default: planning, synthesis, extraction, drafting

Zero cost, zero network transmission, runs on consumer GPU\. Covers 62\.7% of all calls\.

Ollama — Llama 3\.3 70B quantised \(local, optional\)

High\-complexity local reasoning

Full\-capability local model for 48GB\+ VRAM\. No foreign transmission\.

Claude Sonnet 5 / Haiku 4\.5 \(cloud, opt\-in\)

Complex multi\-step planning, policy interpretation

Strongest reasoning for tasks that genuinely need it\. Operator opt\-in; logged in audit trail\.

OpenRouter fallback \(cloud, opt\-in\)

LLM Router failover when primary cloud unavailable

Prevents service interruption\. Operator notified of routing decision\.

CLIP \(open weights, MIT\)

Image understanding in multimodal RAG

Open\-weight, runs locally alongside Ollama\. No API cost\.

Whisper \(open weights, MIT\)

Audio/video transcription in multimodal RAG

Open\-weight, local\. Enables agents to query spoken content\.

IntelliNexus  — graphify CLI

Knowledge graph construction, entity extraction

Local CLI; no external service\. Enables knowledge\.graph\_query primitive\.

## __2\.3 API Layer, Integrations, and IntelliNexus__

All external integrations are exposed as named primitives in the HERMES MCP allowlist\. No agent can call outside this allowlist\. Write operations and all communications require explicit HITL approval\. IntelliNexus feeds the knowledge layer every dataset published through the pipeline is indexed automatically for agent RAG retrieval, enabling the synchronised human\-and\-machine learning model that is core to the intelligent organisation concept\.

__Namespace__

__Key primitives__

__Status__

vault\.\*

search, read, list, write, edit, append, create\_zettel, find\_by\_tag, scan\_docs, write\_pdf

Live

db\.\*

list\_sources, list\_tables, describe\_table, query, write \(HITL\-gated\), schema 1 production DB connected

Live

web\.\*

fetch, scrape, interact, search 3\-tier: direct fetch → Playwright → Firecrawl

Live

github\.\*

list\_repos, read\_repo, get\_file, create\_issue, comment, update, request\_review, list\_issues \(Octokit\)

Live

comms\.\*

email\.send \(Mailjet, multi\-recipient, attachments\), slack\.post, telegram\.post, msteams\.post, webhook\.post

Mailjet live; others env\-gated

payments\.\*

payment\.link \(Stripe\), payment\.paynow\_link, paynow\_poll, status, list

Stripe and Paynow ZW — both live

research\.\*

research\.deep, research\.multiperspective

Live

quality\.\*

quality\.check, security\.scan, peer\.review, peer\.delegate, hermes\.delegate

Live

knowledge\.\*

knowledge\.graph\_query \(graphify / IntelliNexus\), data\.publish, data\.list\_datasets

Live

memory\.\*

memory\.note, memory\.recall, memory\.search, calendar\.activity, calendar\.plan\_day

Live

org\.\*

org\.lookup, org\.escalation\_path, human\.request \(HITL pause/resume\)

Live

skills\.\*

skill\.list, skill\.load, skill\.suggest, skill\.draft, skill\.fetch\_remote — 141 playbooks

Live

## __2\.4 Governance Engine and Policy Enforcement__

Operators upload policy documents \(PDF, Word, or plain text\)\. A local LLM processes each into a structured constraint set: what agents may do, may not do, must do in defined situations, and what data they may include in external outputs\. Constraints are displayed for operator review marked hard \(must never violate\) or soft \(should prefer\)\. Confirmed constraints are injected into agent system prompts and enforced at the HITL gate before any action executes\. The current implementation pre\-execution enforces 20 categories of consequential tool calls against accepted policies\. This is the mechanism that makes the intelligent organisation governable\.

# __Section 3 Deliverables and CCE Implementation Roadmap__

__What is built and demonstrable today__

28 live API routers, all with real handlers and no scaffolding stubs\. ~90 agent primitives across 12 namespaces\. 141 skill playbooks, no orphans\. Governance engine enforcing 20 categories pre\-execution\. Secret\-pattern vault scanner\. Real\-token cost tracker\. 118 unit tests passing\.

## __3\.2 Delivery Streams and Milestones__

__Stream__

__Weeks__

__Deliverable__

__Builds on \(live\)__

B — Onboarding UX

1–3

Sector/language selection, personalised dashboard, Zimbabwe Context panel, Shona/Ndebele i18n strings\. Onboarding flow that makes the intelligent organisation concept legible to a first\-time operator\.

/api/onboarding, Shona/Ndebele glossary

C — Governance UI

3–4

Policy document upload interface\. Constraint review and confirmation screen\. Hard/soft marking\. HITL gate violation flagging in approval interface\. Policy Management page\.

/api/governance, governance engine

D — Localisation

5–6

Shona/Ndebele LLM agent prompt templates\. Five Zimbabwe sector knowledge packs: Tourism, Agriculture, NGO/Civil Society, Health, Government — ingested via IntelliNexus\.

/api/knowledge\-packs, ADRS/IntelliNexus, Qdrant

E — Platform integrity

7–8

Cost monitoring dashboard\. Full mission audit log with replay\. SkillForge operator code\-review step\.

cost\-tracker\.ts, /api/audit, /api/skill\-forge

F — Polish and docs

9–12

Mobile\-responsive console\. Ethics and governance framework document\. Pilot plan\. Compliance documentation\.

Next\.js console \(37 pages live\)

__Milestone__

__Deliverable__

__Compute / dependencies__

__When__

Team hardware: RTX 3060 \(12GB VRAM\) for Ollama 7B\. No ZCHPC CCE required\.

31 July 2026

M1 — Contract signing

Stream B complete: onboarding UX live in staging\. package\-lock\.json dependency manifest locked\.

Week 1 post\-award

Qdrant cloud \(managed\) for shared sector packs\. OpenRouter key for quality testing\.

Week 6 post\-award

M3 — Pilot launch

10–20 pilot organisations onboarded\. First non\-technical operator case study\. Performance dashboard live\.

Hosted: managed PostgreSQL \+ Redis \+ Qdrant\. Est\. USD 40–60/month infrastructure\.

Weeks 7–12 post\-award

## __3\.4 CCE / ZCHPC Position__

NeuroWorks does not require the ZCHPC CCE for primary operation\. The default inference path uses Ollama on the operator's own hardware, a consumer GPU \(RTX 3060, 12GB VRAM\) runs 7B models covering the majority of tasks\. For the Grand Challenge demonstration, the team's own hardware will be used\. For pilot organisations without GPU hardware, ZCHPC access would provide shared local Ollama inference in a controlled, data\-sovereign environment eliminating cloud model routing and its associated data\-transmission risk\. CCE requirements if accessed: 1× GPU node \(16GB\+ VRAM\), 32GB RAM, 100GB storage, outbound access for GitHub API and optional OpenRouter fallback\. All dependencies declared in package\-lock\.json at repository root\.

# __Section 4 Compliance and Risk Mitigation__

Written candidly\. Where a risk is mitigated, the mitigation is named and locatable in the code\. Where it is not yet mitigated, that is stated plainly\.

## __4\.1 Ethical Safeguards__

Hallucination is mitigated at four independent layers: \(1\) anti\-fabrication rules in the synthesis prompt requiring inline evidence citation; \(2\) quality\.check scoring every non\-trivial answer on factuality ri
